wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Dreamer AI short video production platform overview. Explain its purpose as an end-to-end AI-powered video creation system that transforms simple concepts into professional short videos. Document the core value proposition, target audience, and key capabilities including AI script generation, multi-stage video production, and collaborative workflows. Include the technology stack overview covering Vue 3 + Fastify architecture, AI service integrations, and infrastructure components. Provide context on the monorepo structure and explain how the platform addresses pain points in traditional video production workflows. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of the complete workflow from concept to finished product.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend overview documentation for the Vue 3 application. Explain the overall architecture, project structure, and core technologies used. Document the application bootstrap process, main entry point configuration, and global setup procedures. Cover the build system with Vite, development server configuration, and environment setup. Include information about the technology stack choices, dependency management, and project organization principles. Provide an architectural overview showing how different parts of the frontend system work together to deliver the user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for the Dreamer platform. Describe the overall system architecture including the Vue 3 frontend SPA, Fastify backend microservice, shared TypeScript types, and monorepo organization. Document the technology stack choices, component interactions, and system boundaries. Explain how the frontend communicates with the backend through REST APIs, how the backend integrates with external AI services, and how the shared types ensure type safety across packages. Include system context diagrams showing data flow between components, infrastructure dependencies, and deployment topology. Address scalability considerations, security patterns, and the rationale behind architectural decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend overview documentation for the Fastify-based backend service. Document the overall architecture, plugin system, middleware structure, and application initialization process. Explain the modular routing organization, error handling patterns, logging strategies, and configuration management. Include details about the Fastify framework setup, CORS handling, static file serving, and development vs production configurations. Address the plugin architecture for authentication, server-sent events, and custom middleware. Provide an architectural diagram showing component relationships and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the project management system. Detail the project creation workflow, team collaboration features including user invitations and role-based access control, project settings and preferences configuration, and project lifecycle management. Document the project dashboard interface, project templates, and organizational features. Include practical examples of project setup, team member management, and project sharing workflows. Explain the relationship between projects and other entities like episodes and scenes. Address project permissions, ownership transfer, and collaborative editing capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: DeepSeek Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for DeepSeek AI integration covering the complete script generation pipeline. Document the DeepSeek client configuration, API wrapper implementation, and provider pattern for LLM integration. Explain the script parsing and enrichment workflows, including storyboard generation, visual enrichment, and scene prompt optimization. Detail the character identity merging functionality and slot image prompt generation. Include cost management strategies, balance tracking, and error handling mechanisms. Provide examples of prompt engineering patterns, response parsing, and integration with the broader AI pipeline orchestration system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core reusable UI components in the Dreamer frontend. Document the EmptyState component for displaying empty states and loading indicators, StatusBadge component for showing entity statuses and progress tracking, and VideoPlayer component for media playback functionality. Explain component props, events, slots, and styling options. Include usage examples, customization patterns, and integration with the Naive UI design system. Document accessibility features, responsive behavior, and performance considerations for each component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Coding Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive coding standards documentation covering TypeScript development practices, naming conventions, and architectural patterns. Document the layered architecture with clear separation between routes, handlers, services, repositories, and libraries. Explain function design principles including single responsibility, parameter management, and async/await best practices. Cover naming conventions for variables, functions, classes, and files with specific examples. Detail error handling strategies, logging requirements, and type safety practices. Include code review guidelines, dependency injection patterns, and testing approaches. Document frontend development standards including component architecture and state management conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document login, logout, registration, password reset, and session management endpoints. Specify HTTP methods (POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/register, POST /api/auth/logout), request/response schemas with JWT token handling, authentication middleware requirements, and error responses for invalid credentials, expired tokens, and account status issues. Include security considerations, token refresh mechanisms, and rate limiting policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document login, registration, logout, and token refresh endpoints. Specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and JWT token handling. Include authentication middleware requirements, session management, and security considerations. Provide example requests/responses for successful authentication flows and common error scenarios. Document password reset procedures, account verification processes, and multi-factor authentication integration points.

wiki_catalogs.name: BullMQ Configuration and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BullMQ configuration and setup. Document the queue initialization process, connection pooling, and cluster configuration. Explain queue naming conventions, serialization settings, and Redis connection management. Detail the worker registration process, concurrency configuration, and graceful shutdown procedures. Include configuration examples for different environments (development, staging, production). Document queue-specific settings, memory management, and performance tuning parameters. Address connection health checks, reconnection strategies, and failover mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Models and Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for all database tables in the Prisma schema. Document each entity including User, Project, Episode, Scene, Take, Character, Location, Composition, and their relationships. Detail field definitions, data types, constraints, unique indexes, and foreign key relationships. Explain the hierarchical structure from User → Project → Episode → Scene → Take. Document many-to-many relationships like Character ↔ Shot through CharacterShot, and Composition ↔ Scene through CompositionScene. Include relationship cardinalities, cascade behaviors, and referential integrity rules. Provide entity relationship diagrams and explain the business logic behind each relationship.

wiki_catalogs.name: Store Modules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the individual Pinia store modules. Document each store's state structure, actions, getters, and mutations. Explain the project store for content organization, episode store for narrative structure, scene store for generation units, character store for asset management, composition store for final output, and stats store for analytics. Include state initialization patterns, action composition, getter computations, and mutation strategies. Provide examples of store usage in components, state hydration from API responses, and store-specific business logic. Document store boundaries, data normalization patterns, and inter-store dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Logic Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core business logic services that implement the main domain functionality. Document the project management service, episode and scene orchestration, script writing workflows, composition assembly, take generation, and asset management services. Explain service responsibilities, method signatures, parameter validation, and return value structures. Detail business rule enforcement, data transformation patterns, and cross-service interactions. Include usage examples, error handling strategies, and integration patterns with repositories and external APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Domain Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for the core domain entities: User, Project, Episode, Scene, and Take. Detail each entity's fields, data types, constraints, and business rules. Document the hierarchical relationship structure from User → Project → Episode → Scene → Take. Explain field definitions like User.email uniqueness, Project.visualStyle array defaults, Scene.aspectRatio with default '9:16', and Take.status enum values. Include entity relationship diagrams showing cascade behaviors and referential integrity. Provide practical examples of entity creation, querying, and relationship establishment. Document indexing strategies and performance considerations for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Dreamer platform. Cover prerequisites including Node.js >= 18.0.0, pnpm >= 8.0.0, Docker, and FFmpeg requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation, environment configuration, and infrastructure setup. Document the environment variable configuration process with detailed explanations of required API keys for DeepSeek, Wan 2.6, and Seedance 2.0 services. Include practical examples of database initialization, development server startup commands, and accessing the application interfaces. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for initial configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed component architecture documentation for the Vue 3 frontend system. Document the component hierarchy, reusable component patterns, and composition API usage. Explain the distinction between presentational and container components, component lifecycle management, and prop/event communication patterns. Cover the Naive UI integration, custom component development guidelines, and component composition strategies. Include examples of complex components like the StoryboardScriptEditor, VideoPlayer, and status management components. Document component testing approaches, performance optimization techniques, and accessibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for the Vue 3 application. Describe the component-based architecture, Vue Router configuration for navigation, Pinia store for state management, and the overall application structure. Document the integration with Naive UI component library, the build system using Vite, and how the frontend handles API communication. Explain the reactive data flow, component lifecycle management, and user interface patterns. Include details about the development workflow, hot module replacement, and build optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all backend endpoints. Document each route group including authentication (/api/auth), project management (/api/projects), episode management (/api/episodes), character management (/api/characters), location management (/api/locations), shot generation (/api/shots), composition (/api/compositions), pipeline orchestration (/api/pipeline), and task management (/api/tasks). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error responses. Include Swagger/OpenAPI integration, parameter validation, and example requests/responses. Document pagination, filtering, sorting capabilities, and rate limiting where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Script Writing and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the script writing and management system. Cover the AI-powered script generation using DeepSeek API, rich text editor integration with Tiptap, script organization and versioning capabilities, and script approval workflows. Document the script editor interface, formatting options, and collaborative editing features. Include examples of script generation prompts, template usage, and script review processes. Explain the integration with AI services, cost calculation, and quality assessment mechanisms. Address script export formats and integration with video generation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wan 2.6 Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Wan 2.6 AI integration focusing on low-cost video prototype generation. Document the API client implementation, request/response handling, and integration with the image generation service. Explain the prototype generation workflow, quality assessment criteria, and cost optimization strategies. Include configuration options, error handling for AI service failures, and integration patterns with the main video generation pipeline. Provide examples of request formatting, response processing, and quality evaluation metrics used in the prototype generation process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storyboard and Script Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the StoryboardScriptEditor component and related storyboard functionality. Explain the rich text editing capabilities, script formatting options, and real-time collaboration features. Document the component's integration with the Tiptap editor, drag-and-drop scene management, and asset linking functionality. Include examples of script generation workflows, scene breakdown processes, and integration with the AI script generation pipeline. Cover component state management, event handling, and performance optimization for large scripts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed testing strategy documentation covering unit testing, integration testing, and API testing approaches. Document the Vitest testing framework setup, test organization patterns, and mocking strategies. Explain frontend testing with Vue Test Utils, component testing, and state management testing. Cover backend testing including route testing, service testing, and database testing with Prisma. Detail test data management, fixtures, and test environment configuration. Include continuous integration testing workflows, test coverage requirements, and debugging techniques for failing tests. Document performance testing approaches and load testing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for project management endpoints. Document project creation, updates, deletion, and retrieval operations including GET /api/projects, POST /api/projects, PUT /api/projects/{id}, DELETE /api/projects/{id}. Include project import/export functionality, team member management, project settings, and collaboration features. Specify request/response schemas, validation rules, permission requirements, and error handling for project ownership and access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for project management endpoints. Document project creation, retrieval, update, and deletion operations. Specify project configuration options, team member management, permission levels, and project settings. Include request/response schemas for project data structures, member invitation workflows, and collaboration features. Provide examples for project templates, bulk operations, and project status management. Document project hierarchy, nested resource relationships, and access control patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Generation Queue
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the video generation queue system. Document the video generation workflow from scene planning to final output. Explain job creation for prototype generation (Wan 2.6) and refined generation (Seedance 2.0). Detail job prioritization, batch processing, and resource allocation strategies. Document the job lifecycle including creation, processing, retry, and completion phases. Include error handling for AI service failures, timeout management, and partial success scenarios. Explain monitoring metrics, queue statistics, and performance optimization techniques specific to video generation workloads.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Strategy and Schema Evolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed migration documentation for Prisma schema evolution. Document the migration workflow from schema changes to database updates, including prisma migrate dev, prisma migrate deploy, and prisma migrate resolve commands. Explain the migration file structure, timestamp-based ordering, and safe migration practices. Detail rollback procedures, data preservation strategies, and backward compatibility considerations. Document the development vs production migration differences, seed data management, and environment-specific configurations. Include best practices for handling breaking changes, index modifications, and constraint updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for real-time state synchronization mechanisms. Document the SSE bridge implementation for live project updates, including connection management, event parsing, and state reconciliation. Explain the pending image jobs system for tracking asynchronous generation tasks and optimistic UI updates. Cover conflict resolution strategies, offline state handling, and retry mechanisms. Include examples of SSE event handling, state update patterns, and user feedback during long-running operations. Document performance considerations for high-frequency updates and memory management for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AI service integration patterns and implementations. Document the DeepSeek, Wan 2.6, and Seedance service wrappers, LLM factory patterns, and AI response parsing mechanisms. Explain AI provider abstraction, cost calculation, response logging, and error handling strategies. Detail script generation workflows, visual enrichment processes, prompt optimization, and AI model selection criteria. Include configuration management, rate limiting, fallback strategies, and performance monitoring for AI integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed entity model documentation for asset management entities: Character, CharacterImage, Location, and ProjectAsset. Document Character entity with voice configuration and dialogues relationship. Detail CharacterImage hierarchy with parent-child relationships and type enumeration. Explain Location entity with soft deletion capabilities and time-of-day attributes. Document ProjectAsset entity for generic asset management with tagging and mood attributes. Include relationship cardinalities, cascade behaviors, and business logic behind each asset type. Provide examples of asset creation workflows and management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dreamer platform's system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the Vue 3 frontend, Fastify backend, shared TypeScript types, and monorepo organization. Document the technology stack choices, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Explain the microservice-like backend architecture with clear API boundaries, the frontend SPA architecture with Vue Router and Pinia state management, and the shared type system for cross-package communication. Include system context diagrams showing how AI services integrate with the platform, infrastructure dependencies, and deployment topology. Address scalability considerations, security patterns, and the rationale behind architectural decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management documentation for the Pinia-based frontend state system. Document the store architecture, state modules for projects, episodes, scenes, characters, and compositions. Explain state persistence strategies, reactive data patterns, and state synchronization with backend APIs. Cover the SSE bridge implementation for real-time updates, pending job management, and optimistic UI updates. Include examples of complex state operations, async state mutations, and cross-store communication patterns. Document state debugging techniques, performance optimization strategies, and state reset/cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the Fastify-based backend service. Describe the plugin system architecture, middleware pattern, and modular route organization. Document the authentication system using JWT, Server-Sent Events implementation for real-time updates, and the RESTful API design. Explain the Fastify plugin registration pattern, error handling strategies, and logging mechanisms. Include details about CORS configuration, Swagger/OpenAPI documentation, and the overall request/response flow through the backend layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema and ORM
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for the Prisma ORM implementation. Document all entities including users, projects, episodes, scenes, takes, compositions, characters, locations, and their relationships. Detail field definitions, data types, constraints, indexes, and foreign key relationships. Explain the migration strategy, schema evolution process, and data seeding procedures. Include Prisma client usage patterns, query optimization techniques, and transaction handling. Document the repository pattern implementation, database connection management, and performance considerations. Address data validation, soft deletes, audit trails, and data lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the asset management system covering characters, locations, and visual assets. Document character library management including character creation, profile management, and character relationships. Explain location management for setting creation and organization. Detail visual asset storage, retrieval, and versioning systems. Include asset upload workflows, metadata management, and asset categorization. Document the asset library interface, search and filtering capabilities, and bulk operations. Address asset permissions, sharing mechanisms, and integration with video generation pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Seedance 2.0 Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Seedance 2.0 AI integration covering both the API service and Python skill scripts. Document the Seedance API client implementation, request/response handling, and integration patterns. Explain the Python-based skill system including the seedance.py script, multilingual skill support (Chinese/English), and skill execution workflows. Detail the skill configuration, parameter passing, and result processing. Include examples of skill invocation, error handling, and integration with the video refinement pipeline. Document the skill development patterns and customization options for extending Seedance capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Shell Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AuthShell component that manages authentication flows and protected routing. Document the component's role in handling login/logout states, route guards, and authentication context management. Explain the integration with the backend authentication API, JWT token handling, and session persistence. Include examples of protected route implementation, authentication state synchronization, and error handling for authentication failures. Cover component composition patterns and integration with the overall application layout.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development workflow documentation covering the complete development lifecycle. Document the monorepo setup with PNPM workspaces, dependency management, and build processes. Explain the Git workflow including branching strategies, commit conventions, and pull request procedures. Detail the Husky git hooks configuration, pre-commit validation, and quality assurance processes. Cover the CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing, and deployment workflows. Document local development environment setup, debugging techniques, and troubleshooting common development issues. Include release management procedures, versioning strategies, and backward compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Episode & Script API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for episode and script management endpoints. Document episode lifecycle management (GET /api/episodes, POST /api/episodes, PUT /api/episodes/{id}), scene planning (POST /api/scenes, PATCH /api/scenes/{id}), and memory integration (GET /api/memories). Include script generation workflows, AI-assisted writing features, scene breakdown functionality, and narrative structure management. Specify request schemas for script content, scene metadata, and memory associations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Episode and Script API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for episode and script management endpoints. Document episode creation, script generation, scene breakdown, and memory management operations. Specify request/response schemas for narrative structure, script formatting, scene planning, and content organization. Include AI-powered script generation integration, rich text editor capabilities, and version control features. Provide examples for script editing workflows, scene management, and collaborative writing processes. Document content validation, auto-save mechanisms, and conflict resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Processing Queue
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the image processing queue system. Document image upload processing, thumbnail generation, and asset optimization workflows. Explain job types for different image operations including resizing, format conversion, and quality adjustment. Detail batch processing capabilities, progressive enhancement, and memory management during image operations. Document error handling for corrupted images, unsupported formats, and processing failures. Include monitoring techniques for image processing throughput, queue length, and processing time metrics. Address scaling considerations for high-volume image processing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prisma Client Usage and Query Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Prisma client documentation covering usage patterns, query optimization, and repository implementation. Document common query patterns including findUnique, findMany, create, update, delete operations with proper error handling. Explain transaction usage, batch operations, and relationship queries. Detail pagination strategies, sorting, filtering, and complex joins. Document the repository pattern implementation, service layer integration, and dependency injection. Include performance optimization techniques, query profiling, and connection pooling. Address async/await patterns, error handling strategies, and transaction management across multiple entities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Async State Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for asynchronous state management patterns and operations. Document async action patterns, loading state management, error handling strategies, and optimistic update techniques. Explain state hydration from API responses, batch operations, and transaction-like state changes. Cover cancellation patterns, timeout handling, and state rollback mechanisms. Include examples of complex async workflows, parallel operations, and state cleanup procedures. Document performance optimization techniques for large datasets, memoization strategies, and state caching patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Orchestration Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for pipeline orchestration and workflow management services. Document the pipeline orchestrator, executor, and route services that coordinate multi-step video generation workflows. Explain job scheduling, step execution order, dependency resolution, and error recovery mechanisms. Detail script entity parsing pipelines, storyboard generation jobs, and project-level workflow coordination. Include progress tracking, status management, and workflow visualization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Creation Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for content creation entities: Shot, SceneDialogue, and CharacterShot. Detail Shot entity with camera movement and angle attributes for video generation. Document SceneDialogue entity with timing information, voice configuration, and character relationships. Explain CharacterShot many-to-many relationship bridging Shots and CharacterImages. Include field definitions like Shot.order for sequence management and SceneDialogue.start_time_ms for precise timing. Document the creative workflow relationships and provide examples of dialogue scripting and shot planning processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend system documentation for the Fastify-based backend service. Document the modular route organization by domain (auth, projects, episodes, characters, etc.), the layered architecture with service and repository patterns, and the plugin system for middleware and custom functionality. Explain the Prisma ORM integration for database operations, the BullMQ task queue system for background processing, and the AI service integrations. Include detailed coverage of the authentication system, error handling patterns, logging strategies, and API documentation generation. Address the worker architecture for video generation tasks, database schema design, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed routing and navigation documentation for the Vue Router implementation. Document the route configuration, navigation guards, and dynamic route generation. Explain the navigation patterns for different user workflows, programmatic navigation, and route parameter handling. Cover authentication-based routing, breadcrumb navigation, and deep linking support. Include examples of complex navigation scenarios, route meta properties, and navigation optimization techniques. Document the relationship between routes and views, lazy loading strategies, and navigation performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data flow patterns and communication mechanisms throughout the Dreamer platform. Describe how data moves between frontend and backend, the role of shared TypeScript types in maintaining consistency, and the API contract design. Document the request/response patterns, error propagation, and data transformation processes. Explain how the frontend consumes backend APIs, how the backend processes requests through its layered architecture, and how shared types ensure type safety across package boundaries. Include examples of typical data flow scenarios and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Task Queue and Workers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the BullMQ task queue system and worker architecture. Document the queue configuration for video generation, image processing, and import operations. Explain worker registration, job processing patterns, retry mechanisms, and error handling strategies. Detail the task lifecycle from creation to completion, including job prioritization, concurrency limits, and resource management. Document the AI service workers for DeepSeek, Wan 2.6, and Seedance integration. Include monitoring and debugging techniques, queue statistics, and performance optimization. Address scaling considerations, distributed processing, and failure recovery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Generation Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the multi-stage video generation pipeline. Detail the scene planning and organization system, take generation and quality assessment, and composition editing capabilities. Document the integration with AI services including Wan 2.6 for prototyping and Seedance 2.0 for refinement. Explain the take selection process, quality comparison features, and final composition assembly. Include examples of pipeline configuration, progress tracking, and error handling. Address the timeline editing interface, audio integration, and final output generation. Document the worker queue system, background processing, and real-time status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Workflow Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AI workflow orchestration covering the factory pattern for LLM providers, API call management, and logging systems. Document the LLM factory implementation for dynamic provider selection, the provider abstraction layer, and unified API call service. Explain the model call logging, API request/response tracking, and error handling strategies. Include configuration management for different AI providers, rate limiting, retry mechanisms, and performance monitoring. Detail the workflow coordination between different AI services, batch processing capabilities, and resource optimization techniques for efficient AI service utilization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contribution Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop contribution guidelines documentation covering community participation and code contribution processes. Document the code review process, pull request requirements, and approval workflows. Explain the issue reporting process, bug tracking, and feature request procedures. Cover the onboarding process for new contributors, mentorship opportunities, and knowledge transfer practices. Detail the community standards, communication guidelines, and collaboration best practices. Include recognition programs, contributor acknowledgments, and long-term contributor pathways. Document the governance model, decision-making processes, and conflict resolution mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for asset management endpoints covering characters and locations. Document character creation and management (POST /api/characters, GET /api/characters), location management (POST /api/locations, GET /api/locations), character image handling (POST /api/character-images), and character shot generation (POST /api/character-shots). Include asset upload/download, metadata management, versioning, and storage integration with MinIO. Specify file upload limits, supported formats, and asset organization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for asset management endpoints covering characters, locations, and visual assets. Document asset upload, retrieval, update, and deletion operations. Specify media handling for images, videos, and audio files. Include asset metadata management, categorization systems, and search functionality. Provide examples for asset versioning, batch operations, and integration with storage services. Document asset validation, format restrictions, and performance optimization for large media files.

wiki_catalogs.name: Import Data Queue
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the data import queue system for bulk operations and data migration. Explain job types for importing projects, scenes, characters, and other entities. Detail validation processes, data transformation pipelines, and conflict resolution strategies. Document batch size optimization, progress tracking, and user notification systems. Include error handling for malformed data, constraint violations, and partial failures. Explain rollback mechanisms, audit logging, and data integrity verification. Address performance considerations for large-scale imports and concurrent processing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Validation and Security Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create data validation and security documentation for the Prisma schema implementation. Document field-level validation rules, data type constraints, and input sanitization requirements. Explain soft delete patterns using deletedAt fields, audit trail implementation, and data lifecycle management. Detail security considerations including SQL injection prevention, data masking, and access control patterns. Document enum usage for status fields, array constraints for tags and styles, and JSON field validation. Include data export/import procedures, backup strategies, and disaster recovery considerations. Address GDPR compliance, data retention policies, and user data portability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage and Media Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for storage management and media processing services. Document the storage service abstraction, FFmpeg integration, composition export workflows, and queue management for media generation. Explain file upload/download patterns, object storage integration, media format conversion, and batch processing workflows. Detail queue service implementations for video and image generation, job prioritization, and resource management. Include performance optimization, caching strategies, and storage cost management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Orchestration Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed entity model documentation for pipeline orchestration entities: Composition, CompositionScene, PipelineJob, and PipelineStepResult. Document Composition entity for final video assembly with status tracking and output URLs. Detail CompositionScene bridge table for timeline assembly linking compositions, scenes, and takes. Explain PipelineJob entity with job types, current step tracking, and progress metadata. Document PipelineStepResult for granular step execution tracking and error handling. Include workflow state machines, progress calculation logic, and error recovery mechanisms. Provide examples of pipeline execution monitoring and troubleshooting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend system documentation for the Vue 3 application. Document the component architecture using Naive UI, the Pinia state management system, Vue Router configuration, and the API integration layer. Explain the reactive data flow, component composition patterns, and user interface design principles. Include coverage of the rich text editor integration, real-time collaboration features, and responsive design implementation. Document the build configuration with Vite, development workflow, and deployment considerations. Address performance optimization techniques, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API integration documentation for the frontend backend communication layer. Document the HTTP client configuration, authentication integration, and error handling strategies. Explain the API service architecture, request/response transformation, and caching mechanisms. Cover the SSE integration for real-time updates, authentication token management, and API versioning. Include examples of complex API operations, batch requests, and offline-first patterns. Document error boundary implementation, retry strategies, and API performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure and Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create infrastructure and dependency documentation for the Dreamer platform's deployment environment. Describe the Docker orchestration setup, service dependencies, and container networking. Document the PostgreSQL database configuration, Prisma ORM setup, Redis for caching and queues, and MinIO for object storage. Explain the health checks, scaling considerations, and environment variable configuration. Include details about the AI service integrations, external API dependencies, and how the infrastructure supports the platform's video generation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the backend system. Document JWT token generation and validation, user registration and login processes, password hashing and security measures. Explain role-based access control (RBAC), permission systems, and resource-level authorization. Detail session management, token refresh mechanisms, and logout procedures. Include middleware implementation for protecting routes, API key authentication, and third-party integration authentication. Document security best practices, rate limiting, brute force protection, and audit logging. Address CORS configuration, secure cookie handling, and CSRF protection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Collaboration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the real-time collaboration system. Document the Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementation for live updates, concurrent editing support, and conflict resolution mechanisms. Explain the socket-based communication system, presence indicators, and collaborative editing features. Include examples of real-time notifications, live commenting, and shared workspace functionality. Document the technical implementation of event broadcasting, client-side synchronization, and offline handling. Address performance considerations, scalability aspects, and debugging tools for real-time features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for video generation and composition endpoints. Document shot management (POST /api/shots, GET /api/shots), take generation and selection (POST /api/takes, PATCH /api/takes/{id}), composition assembly (POST /api/compositions), and image generation jobs (GET /api/image-generation-jobs). Include AI model integration, generation parameters, quality assessment, timeline editing, and final output generation. Specify request schemas for generation configurations, progress tracking, and result retrieval.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for video generation and composition endpoints. Document shot creation, take generation, composition assembly, and image generation job management. Specify request/response schemas for video processing parameters, quality settings, and generation workflows. Include AI model integration points, batch processing capabilities, and progress tracking mechanisms. Provide examples for video editing operations, timeline assembly, and export formats. Document error handling, retry mechanisms, and cost calculation for AI service usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Worker Management and Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for worker management and monitoring systems. Document worker registration, lifecycle management, and health monitoring. Explain worker scaling strategies, load balancing, and resource allocation. Detail monitoring techniques including queue statistics, worker performance metrics, and error tracking. Document alerting systems, log aggregation, and debugging tools. Include troubleshooting guides for common worker issues, memory leaks, and performance bottlenecks. Address graceful shutdown procedures, worker replacement strategies, and maintenance windows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for authentication, authorization, and user management services. Document the authentication service implementation, JWT token management, session handling, and user registration workflows. Explain authorization patterns, role-based access control, permission checking, and security middleware integration. Detail user settings management, statistics collection, and audit logging services. Include security best practices, token refresh mechanisms, and account management features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory System Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for the memory system entities: MemoryItem and MemorySnapshot. Document MemoryItem with type enumeration covering CHARACTER, LOCATION, EVENT, PLOT_POINT, FORESHADOWING, RELATIONSHIP, and VISUAL_STYLE categories. Detail metadata structure for episode associations and importance scoring. Explain MemorySnapshot entity for project timeline snapshots with summary generation and context JSON storage. Include vector embedding fields for future AI-powered search capabilities. Document the relationship between memory items and project episodes, providing examples of memory creation, categorization, and retrieval workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI integration documentation covering the three-tier AI pipeline. Document the DeepSeek API integration for script generation, the Wan 2.6 API for low-cost prototype generation, and the Seedance 2.0 API for high-quality refinement. Explain the cost calculation and management strategies, API key configuration, and error handling for AI service failures. Include detailed coverage of the Python-based skill scripts for Seedance integration, prompt engineering best practices, and quality assessment criteria. Document the AI workflow orchestration, batch processing capabilities, and performance optimization techniques for AI service utilization.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed UI design system documentation for the frontend visual framework. Document the styling architecture, CSS variables, and design token system. Explain the Naive UI component integration, custom component theming, and responsive design implementation. Cover the color palette, typography system, spacing guidelines, and component variants. Include examples of design system usage, theme customization, and accessibility compliance. Document the image handling system, character asset management, and visual consistency patterns across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the service layer architecture and business logic implementation. Document the service pattern implementation, dependency injection, and service composition patterns. Explain the repository pattern for data access, unit of work patterns, and transaction management. Detail service orchestration, cross-service communication, and error propagation. Include business rule enforcement, validation strategies, and data transformation patterns. Document service testing approaches, mocking strategies, and integration testing. Address performance optimization, caching strategies, and service monitoring. Explain the relationship between services, repositories, and external APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the pipeline orchestration system. Detail the workflow management for coordinating video generation tasks, step-by-step processing logic, and progress tracking mechanisms. Document the task queue integration with BullMQ, worker coordination, and error handling and recovery procedures. Explain the pipeline configuration, custom workflow creation, and monitoring capabilities. Include examples of pipeline execution, status reporting, and performance optimization. Address pipeline scaling, resource management, and integration with external AI services. Document the retry mechanisms, timeout handling, and failure recovery strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline & Task API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for pipeline orchestration and task management endpoints. Document pipeline execution (POST /api/pipeline/start, GET /api/pipeline/status), task queue management (GET /api/tasks, POST /api/tasks), and model API call tracking (GET /api/model-api-calls). Include workflow coordination, step-by-step processing, progress monitoring, error handling, retry mechanisms, and resource allocation. Specify pipeline configuration, task prioritization, and monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for pipeline orchestration and task management endpoints. Document workflow initiation, progress monitoring, task queuing, and pipeline state management. Specify request/response schemas for pipeline configuration, step execution, and error handling. Include real-time progress tracking, job scheduling, and resource allocation. Provide examples for pipeline customization, parallel processing, and failure recovery mechanisms. Document task prioritization, dependency management, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility and Helper Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document utility and helper services that provide supporting functionality across the application. Explain the memory service for context management, prompt template engine, action extraction utilities, storyboard generation helpers, data importers, and task management services. Detail helper function implementations, reusable algorithms, and cross-cutting concerns. Include configuration management, logging utilities, and debugging support services.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed entity model documentation for API integration entities: ModelApiCall and ImportTask. Document ModelApiCall entity with user association, provider tracking, and response data storage. Detail field definitions like external_task_id for tracking AI service requests and cost calculation fields. Explain ImportTask entity for content import workflows with status tracking and result storage. Include API call logging patterns, error handling strategies, and cost management approaches. Document the relationship between API calls and generated takes, providing examples of AI service integration and monitoring workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Database
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Prisma-based database schema. Detail the entity relationships including User, Project, Episode, Scene, Take, Composition, Character, and Location models. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules. Explain the primary/foreign key relationships, indexing strategies, and query optimization patterns. Include database schema diagrams, sample data structures, and common access patterns. Document the Prisma migration strategy, data lifecycle management, and backup procedures. Address data security considerations, access control patterns, and performance optimization techniques for large-scale video production data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive error handling and logging documentation for the backend system. Document the custom HTTP error classes, error response formatting, and error propagation patterns. Explain structured logging implementation, log levels, and log aggregation strategies. Detail error tracking, monitoring integration, and alerting mechanisms. Include debugging techniques, stack trace handling, and sensitive data sanitization. Document graceful degradation strategies, circuit breaker patterns, and fallback mechanisms. Address performance monitoring, error rate tracking, and system health checks. Explain integration with external monitoring tools and log analysis platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Settings API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for system configuration and statistics endpoints. Document settings management (GET /api/settings, PUT /api/settings) for system-wide configuration, user preferences, and feature flags. Include statistics and analytics endpoints (GET /api/stats) for usage metrics, performance monitoring, and system health reporting. Specify configuration schemas, validation rules, and administrative access requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for system integration endpoints including model API calls, statistics, settings, and import/export operations. Document analytics data collection, system configuration, and data migration interfaces. Specify request/response schemas for monitoring metrics, usage statistics, and administrative functions. Include examples for system health checks, performance monitoring, and configuration management. Document API rate limiting, data export formats, and integration with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed feature implementation documentation covering the complete workflow from project creation to video export. Document the project management features including team collaboration and settings, script writing with AI assistance, asset management for characters and locations, and the multi-stage video generation pipeline. Explain the scene and take management system, composition and editing capabilities, and real-time collaboration features. Include practical examples of API usage, component integration, and user workflow patterns. Address the pipeline orchestration, progress tracking, and quality control mechanisms throughout the video production process.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the Dreamer platform. Document authentication endpoints, project management APIs, episode and script management endpoints, character and asset management APIs, video generation endpoints, and pipeline management interfaces. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error codes. Include detailed parameter descriptions, example requests/responses, and common usage patterns. Document the Swagger/OpenAPI integration, rate limiting policies, and API versioning strategy. Provide client implementation guidelines and integration examples for common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation covering coding standards, testing strategies, and development workflow. Document the TypeScript coding standards, naming conventions, and code organization principles. Explain the testing approach including unit testing, integration testing, and API testing strategies. Cover the development workflow including branching strategies, code review processes, and CI/CD pipeline configuration. Document the Husky git hooks, pre-commit validation, and quality assurance processes. Include contribution guidelines, release management procedures, and debugging techniques for the complex monorepo environment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for the Dreamer platform. Document the Docker orchestration setup including service dependencies, health checks, and scaling considerations. Explain the infrastructure requirements for PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO, and FFmpeg integration. Cover the production deployment strategies, environment configuration management, and monitoring setup. Document the backup and recovery procedures, performance optimization techniques, and troubleshooting common operational issues. Include capacity planning guidelines, security hardening measures, and disaster recovery procedures for the AI-intensive video production workload.

wiki_items.title: Prisma Client Usage and Query Patterns

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Dreamer - AI Short Video Production Platform

## Project Introduction

Dreamer is an innovative AI-powered short video production platform that transforms creative ideas into finished products through an end-to-end workflow. Built as a modern monorepo architecture, it serves as a comprehensive solution for AI-driven short video creation, from initial concept to final delivery.

### Purpose Statement
The platform enables creators to transform simple concepts ("one-line ideas") into professional short videos through AI-assisted production workflows, providing full control and intervention capabilities throughout the entire production pipeline.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **End-to-End Automation**: Streamline the complete journey from script to finished video
- **AI Integration**: Leverage multiple AI models for different stages of production
- **User Control**: Maintain human oversight and intervention capabilities
- **API-First Design**: Provide robust APIs for programmatic access and automation
- **Scalable Architecture**: Support both individual creators and team-based production

### Target Audience
- Content creators and influencers seeking efficient short video production
- Marketing teams requiring rapid content generation
- Educational institutions teaching digital media production
- Independent filmmakers exploring AI-assisted workflows

## Technical Architecture

The Dreamer platform follows a modern monorepo architecture with clear separation of concerns across frontend, backend, and shared components.

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        FE[Vite + Vue 3 Application]
        UI[Naive UI Components]
        State[Pinia State Management]
        Router[Vue Router Navigation]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        API[Fastify REST API]
        Auth[Authentication System]
        Queue[BullMQ Task Queue]
        Plugins[Fastify Plugins]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        DB[(PostgreSQL Database)]
        Redis[(Redis Cache)]
        S3[(MinIO Storage)]
    end
    
    subgraph "AI Services"
        DeepSeek[DeepSeek API]
        Wan26[Wan 2.6 API]
        Seedance[Seedance 2.0 API]
    end
    
    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        FFmpeg[FFmpeg Processing]
        Docker[Docker Compose]
    end
    
    FE --> API
    API --> Auth
    API --> Queue
    Queue --> Workers[Video Generation Workers]
    API --> DB
    API --> Redis
    API --> S3
    Workers --> DeepSeek
    Workers --> Wan26
    Workers --> Seedance
    Workers --> FFmpeg
    API --> Docker
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture**
- Vue 3 with TypeScript for reactive UI components
- Naive UI for enterprise-grade component library
- Pinia for centralized state management
- Vue Router for SPA navigation
- Custom Tiptap-based rich text editor for script editing

**Backend Architecture**
- Fastify microservice framework for high-performance API
- Prisma ORM for database abstraction
- BullMQ for distributed task queue management
- JWT-based authentication system
- Swagger/OpenAPI documentation

**Shared Layer**
- TypeScript interfaces defining the complete data model
- Cross-project type definitions and utilities
- Common validation schemas and utilities

### Design Patterns

The platform implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Monorepo Pattern**: Single codebase managing multiple packages
- **API-First Design**: Clear separation between frontend and backend
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Task queues for asynchronous processing
- **Repository Pattern**: Database abstraction through Prisma
- **Plugin Architecture**: Extensible Fastify middleware system

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**
The primary backend entry point is located at `packages/backend/src/index.ts`, which initializes the Fastify server, registers all plugins, and mounts the complete routing system.

**Frontend Entry Point**
The Vue application bootstraps from `packages/frontend/src/main.ts`, setting up the application instance with Pinia, Vue Router, and Naive UI integration.

**Shared Types Definition**
The shared types system provides comprehensive type safety across the entire platform, defined in `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts`.

### Core Modules

**API Route Structure**
The backend implements a comprehensive routing system covering all major domain areas:
- Authentication routes (`/api/auth`)
- Project management routes (`/api/projects`)
- Episode and script management (`/api/episodes`)
- Character and asset management (`/api/characters`)
- Video generation and composition (`/api/shots`, `/api/compositions`)
- Pipeline orchestration (`/api/pipeline`)

**Task Queue Management**
The platform uses BullMQ for handling long-running video generation tasks, supporting both synchronous API calls and asynchronous background processing.

**Database Schema**
Prisma manages the complete data model with support for:
- User authentication and authorization
- Project lifecycle management
- Character and asset libraries
- Episode and scene tracking
- Video generation metadata
- Pipeline state management

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
The platform supports extensive environment variable configuration for different deployment scenarios, including database connections, AI service credentials, and infrastructure settings.

**Docker Orchestration**
Complete infrastructure provisioning through Docker Compose, including PostgreSQL, Redis, and MinIO services with health checks and automated bucket setup.

**Build System**
PNPM workspaces manage the monorepo structure with parallel development and build processes across all packages.

### External Dependencies

**Core Technology Stack**
- **Frontend**: Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite, Naive UI, Pinia, Vue Router
- **Backend**: Node.js, Fastify, Prisma, BullMQ, Redis, PostgreSQL
- **AI Services**: DeepSeek, Wan 2.6, Seedance 2.0
- **Infrastructure**: Docker, FFmpeg, MinIO

**Integration Points**
- AWS SDK for S3-compatible storage
- OpenAI client for model interactions
- Fastify ecosystem plugins for CORS, JWT, and file uploads
- Prisma for database operations

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class User {
        +string id
        +string email
        +string name
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
    }
    
    class Project {
        +string id
        +string name
        +string? description
        +string? synopsis
        +string? aspectRatio
        +Array~string~ visualStyle
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
    }
    
    class Episode {
        +string id
        +string projectId
        +number episodeNum
        +string? title
        +string? synopsis
        +ScriptContent? script
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
    }
    
    class Character {
        +string id
        +string projectId
        +string name
        +string? description
        +Array~CharacterImage~ images
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
    }
    
    class Take {
        +string id
        +string sceneId
        +VideoModel model
        +TaskStatus status
        +string prompt
        +number? cost
        +number? duration
        +string? videoUrl
        +string? thumbnailUrl
        +boolean isSelected
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
    }
    
    User "1" --> "many" Project
    Project "1" --> "many" Episode
    Episode "1" --> "many" Take
    Project "1" --> "many" Character
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**AI-Powered Script Generation**
The platform leverages DeepSeek AI to transform simple concepts into structured, production-ready scripts with detailed scene breakdowns and character assignments.

**Multi-Stage Video Generation**
Through integration with Wan 2.6 and Seedance 2.0, the system supports three distinct generation phases:
1. Initial rough cuts using Wan 2.6 for rapid prototyping
2. High-quality refinement with Seedance 2.0
3. Final compositing and post-production enhancements

**Comprehensive Asset Management**
Centralized management of characters, locations, and visual assets with support for multiple variations and versions.

**Real-time Collaboration**
SSE (Server-Sent Events) enable real-time updates and collaborative workflows across team members.

### Implementation Highlights

**Pipeline Orchestration**
The `packages/backend/src/routes/pipeline.ts` implements sophisticated workflow management coordinating multiple AI services and manual review steps.

**Task Queue System**
BullMQ-based worker architecture handles video generation tasks with automatic retry, monitoring, and progress tracking.

**Rich Text Editor Integration**
Custom Tiptap-based editor provides advanced script editing capabilities with real-time collaboration features.

**Responsive Frontend Architecture**
Vue 3 components with modular design patterns ensure maintainable and scalable frontend codebase.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Concept: User Input
    Concept --> ScriptGeneration: AI Processing
    ScriptGeneration --> EpisodePlanning: Structured Script
    EpisodePlanning --> AssetPreparation: Scene Breakdown
    AssetPreparation --> VideoGeneration: Asset Ready
    VideoGeneration --> QualityReview: Generated Clips
    QualityReview --> Composition: Approved Clips
    Composition --> FinalOutput: Timeline Assembly
    FinalOutput --> [*]: Delivered Video
    
    VideoGeneration --> VideoGeneration: Retry on Failure
    QualityReview --> EpisodePlanning: Revisions Needed
```

The platform represents a sophisticated example of modern full-stack development, combining cutting-edge AI technologies with robust engineering practices to deliver a professional-grade content creation solution.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [pnpm-workspace.yaml](pnpm-workspace.yaml)
- [packages/backend/package.json](packages/backend/package.json)
- [packages/frontend/package.json](packages/frontend/package.json)
- [packages/shared/package.json](packages/shared/package.json)
- [packages/backend/src/index.ts](packages/backend/src/index.ts)
- [packages/frontend/src/main.ts](packages/frontend/src/main.ts)
- [packages/shared/src/types/index.ts](packages/shared/src/types/index.ts)
- [docker/docker-compose.yml](docker/docker-compose.yml)
- [README.md](README.md)

</blog>
